_about: Option-1 F3 multi-disease stress topics. TWO comorbidity clinical-AI topics, each written twice (condition-forward CONTROL + mechanism-first). Unlike the F1/F2 topics (one disease each), these deliberately mention TWO real diseases so the wide-net engine surfaces MORE THAN ONE resolvable MeSH heading -> a genuine disambiguation fork. Each topic marks the PRIMARY oracle (the condition the validation spec should actually be about = the target the model detects/predicts) and a SECONDARY oracle (a comorbidity / population / downstream-risk condition that also surfaces but is NOT the spec's subject). Design intent: in the CONTROL phrasing the use_case NAMES the primary target, so a ranking rule that prefers the use_case-named / full-bigram disease should pick it cleanly with NO clarifying-question pop-up; in the MECHANISM_FIRST phrasing the use_case names NEITHER disease (it describes the mechanism/workflow), so the fork is genuinely ambiguous -> the F3 clarifying-question SHOULD fire. This is the case F2 could not exercise (F2 topics surfaced only one disease each). Used by f3_probe.py (enumerate all diseases the wide-net surfaces + what naive first-resolve picks) and, after, by the F3 ranking-rule / pop-up build.

topics.condition_lay: acute heart failure decompensation in patients with type 2 diabetes
topics.mesh_note: Two diseases both resolve to real C-tree headings: Heart Failure (D006333, the predicted TARGET = primary) and Diabetes Mellitus / Type 2 (D003920 / D003924, the population comorbidity = secondary). In mechanism_first the model_desc mentions 'type 2 diabetes mellitus' BEFORE 'heart failure', so naive first-resolve-in-text-order risks picking Diabetes (wrong). The spec is about predicting heart failure, so Heart Failure is the intended subject.

control.model_desc: A clinical decision-support tool that predicts acute heart failure decompensation in patients with type 2 diabetes.
control.use_case: early prediction of heart failure decompensation to guide escalation of diuretic therapy
control.population: adults with type 2 diabetes mellitus and a prior heart failure diagnosis

mechanism_first.model_desc: A gradient-boosted ensemble model that ingests longitudinal EHR vitals, glycemic laboratory time-series, and medication-administration records to output a continuously-updated deterioration risk score, developed and evaluated in a cohort with comorbid type 2 diabetes mellitus and chronic heart failure.
mechanism_first.population: adults managed in an integrated health system

topics.condition_lay: atrial fibrillation detection to prevent embolic stroke
topics.mesh_note: Two diseases both resolve to real C-tree headings: Atrial Fibrillation (D001281, the DETECTED target = primary) and Stroke (D020521, the downstream risk the tool aims to prevent = secondary). In mechanism_first the model_desc mentions 'embolic stroke' BEFORE 'atrial fibrillation', so naive first-resolve-in-text-order risks picking Stroke (wrong). The spec is about detecting AF, so Atrial Fibrillation is the intended subject.

control.model_desc: An algorithm that detects atrial fibrillation from single-lead ECG to prevent embolic stroke.

mechanism_first.model_desc: A deep neural network that classifies single-lead ECG waveform morphology to identify irregular rhythm segments, developed to reduce embolic stroke through earlier initiation of anticoagulation in patients with atrial fibrillation.
